audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for clean/unclean animals, ark entry, flood chronology, mabbul waters, breath of life, and blotting-out language. Final source-critical review may refine flood chronology and numeric traditions.

verses.restored: And the Eternal said to Noah, Come, you and all your house, into the ark, for I have seen you righteous before me in this generation.

familiar.text: And the LORD said unto Noah, Come thou and all thy house into the ark; for thee have I seen righteous before me in this generation.

notes.title: Come into the ark
notes.body: The command is not merely to build but to enter the vessel of preservation.

verses.restored: From every clean animal you shall take for yourself seven pairs, male and female; and from animals that are not clean, two, male and female.

familiar.text: Of every clean beast thou shalt take to thee by sevens, the male and his female: and of beasts that are not clean by two, the male and his female.

notes.title: Clean and not clean
notes.body: Clean/unclean categories appear before Sinai. The text anticipates later ritual distinctions without explaining them fully here.

verses.restored: Also from the winged creatures of the heavens, seven pairs, male and female, to keep seed alive upon the face of all the earth.

familiar.text: Of fowls also of the air by sevens, the male and the female; to keep seed alive upon the face of all the earth.

verses.restored: For in seven more days I will send rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights, and I will wipe away every standing thing that I have made from the face of the ground.

familiar.text: For yet seven days, and I will cause it to rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights; and every living substance that I have made will I destroy from off the face of the earth.

notes.title: Wipe away
notes.body: Machah repeats the severe blotting-out language from Genesis 6.

verses.restored: And Noah did according to all that the Eternal commanded him.

familiar.text: And Noah did according unto all that the LORD commanded him.

verses.restored: Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters came upon the earth.

familiar.text: And Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters was upon the earth.

verses.restored: Noah came into the ark, and his sons, his woman, and his sons’ women with him, from before the waters of the flood.

familiar.text: And Noah went in, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons' wives with him, into the ark, because of the waters of the flood.

verses.restored: From the clean animals, from the animals that were not clean, from the winged creatures, and from everything that creeps upon the ground,

familiar.text: Of clean beasts, and of beasts that are not clean, and of fowls, and of every thing that creepeth upon the earth,

verses.restored: two by two they came to Noah into the ark, male and female, as the Divine had commanded Noah.

familiar.text: There went in two and two unto Noah into the ark, the male and the female, as God had commanded Noah.

verses.restored: After seven days, the waters of the flood came upon the earth.

familiar.text: And it came to pass after seven days, that the waters of the flood were upon the earth.

verses.restored: In the six hundredth year of Noah’s life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day of the month, on that day all the fountains of the great deep burst open, and the windows of the heavens were opened.

familiar.text: In the six hundredth year of Noah's life, in the second month, the seventeenth day of the month... were all the fountains of the great deep broken up, and the windows of heaven were opened.

notes.title: Deep and heavens opened
notes.body: The flood is described as waters from below and above, undoing the ordered separations of Genesis 1.

verses.restored: And the rain was upon the earth forty days and forty nights.

familiar.text: And the rain was upon the earth forty days and forty nights.

verses.restored: On that very day Noah entered the ark, with Shem, Ham, and Japheth, Noah’s sons, and Noah’s woman, and the three women of his sons with them.

familiar.text: In the selfsame day entered Noah, and Shem, and Ham, and Japheth... and Noah's wife, and the three wives of his sons with them, into the ark;

verses.restored: they and every living being according to its kind, every animal according to its kind, every creeping thing that creeps on the earth according to its kind, and every winged creature according to its kind, every bird, every winged thing.

familiar.text: They, and every beast after his kind... and every fowl after his kind, every bird of every sort.

verses.restored: They came to Noah into the ark, two by two from all flesh in which was the breath of life.

familiar.text: And they went in unto Noah into the ark, two and two of all flesh, wherein is the breath of life.

verses.restored: Those that came, male and female of all flesh, came as the Divine had commanded him. And the Eternal shut him in.

familiar.text: And they that went in, went in male and female of all flesh... and the LORD shut him in.

notes.title: Shut him in
notes.body: The Eternal closes the ark. Preservation is sealed by divine action.

verses.restored: The flood was forty days upon the earth. The waters increased and lifted the ark, and it rose above the earth.

familiar.text: And the flood was forty days upon the earth; and the waters increased, and bare up the ark...

verses.restored: The waters prevailed and increased greatly upon the earth, and the ark moved upon the face of the waters.

familiar.text: And the waters prevailed, and were increased greatly upon the earth; and the ark went upon the face of the waters.

verses.restored: The waters prevailed very greatly upon the earth, and all the high mountains under all the heavens were covered.

familiar.text: And the waters prevailed exceedingly upon the earth; and all the high hills... were covered.

verses.restored: Fifteen cubits upward the waters prevailed, and the mountains were covered.

familiar.text: Fifteen cubits upward did the waters prevail; and the mountains were covered.

verses.restored: All flesh that moved on the earth expired: winged creatures, animals, living beings, every swarming thing that swarms on the earth, and all humanity.

familiar.text: And all flesh died that moved upon the earth... and every creeping thing... and every man:

verses.restored: Everything that had the breath of the spirit of life in its nostrils, everything on dry ground, died.

familiar.text: All in whose nostrils was the breath of life, of all that was in the dry land, died.

notes.title: Breath in nostrils
notes.body: The phrase recalls the creation of the human in Genesis 2:7.

verses.restored: He wiped away every standing thing that was on the face of the ground, from human to animal, creeping thing, and winged creature of the heavens. They were wiped away from the earth. Only Noah was left, and those with him in the ark.

familiar.text: And every living substance was destroyed which was upon the face of the ground... and Noah only remained alive, and they that were with him in the ark.

notes.title: Only Noah was left
notes.body: The flood narrative narrows life to the preserved remnant inside the ark.

verses.restored: And the waters prevailed upon the earth one hundred fifty days.

familiar.text: And the waters prevailed upon the earth an hundred and fifty days.
